# Story 1.2: Mobile Login First Screen

Status: done

<!-- Generated by BMAD 6.8 create-story workflow on 2026-06-17. -->

## Story

As a traveler,
I want a mobile-first login screen with equal entry options and privacy links,
so that I can enter the app with clear trust signals.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given the mobile app shell exists in the monorepo, when the login screen renders, then it follows the UX spec for mobile-only single-column layout, touch targets >=44pt, and 120-200ms transitions.
2. Given Apple, phone, and WeChat login options are enabled, when the screen renders on iOS, then all enabled options are equal size and ordered Apple, phone, WeChat.
3. Given a user chooses phone login, when they submit phone and OTP, then the screen uses the backend auth contract from Story 1.1 and handles captcha-required, retry-after, success, and session-expired states.
4. Given the user wants compliance details, when they tap privacy or user agreement links, then the links open without blocking the login flow.
5. Given login first-screen analytics are required, when the screen is viewed or a login method is tapped, then semantic analytics events are emitted without collecting phone numbers, OTPs, cookies, or other PII.
6. Given the backend or network is unavailable, when config, OTP start, OTP verify, or `/me` fails, then the UI shows a recoverable error state and does not fake an authenticated session.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Create the mobile app shell under `apps/mobile` (AC: 1, 6)
  - [x] Use a mobile Web implementation with Vite + React + TypeScript unless an existing mobile package appears before implementation starts.
  - [x] Add `apps/mobile/package.json` with `nomad-mobile` scripts for `dev`, `build`, `preview`, and `typecheck` or equivalent.
  - [x] Keep the package inside the existing pnpm workspace; do not create a Windows-side project and do not copy from the old migration image.
  - [x] Configure the app to consume the backend through `VITE_API_BASE_URL`, defaulting to the local Fastify server origin used by development.
- [x] Implement login first-screen UI (AC: 1, 2, 4)
  - [x] Fetch `/auth/config` on load and render enabled methods from `enabled_methods`.
  - [x] On iOS-style rendering, apply `ios_equal_weight_order` so Apple, phone, and WeChat are equal size and ordered Apple, phone, WeChat whenever those methods are enabled.
  - [x] Use a single-column mobile layout with stable controls, no nested card layout, and no desktop-only split view.
  - [x] Keep all interactive targets at least 44x44pt and transitions in the 120-200ms range.
  - [x] Show minimal brand treatment plus first-screen privacy and user-agreement links from `privacy_url` and `user_agreement_url`.
  - [x] Open privacy and user-agreement links in a non-blocking browser/new-window flow; returning to login must preserve the current phone/OTP state.
- [x] Implement phone OTP flow against Story 1.1 contract (AC: 3, 6)
  - [x] Submit `POST /auth/otp/start` with `{ phone, region: "CN" }` and optional `captcha_token`.
  - [x] Respect `retry_after_sec` and 429 `Retry-After` by disabling resend and showing cooldown.
  - [x] If `captcha_required=true`, show a verification-required state and retry OTP start only after receiving a captcha token; in local development the backend stub token is `captcha-ok`.
  - [x] Submit `POST /auth/otp/verify` with `{ phone, otp, device_fingerprint }`; `code` is only a compatibility alias and should not be the primary client field.
  - [x] Use `credentials: "include"` for auth requests so the httpOnly `sid` cookie is set and sent.
  - [x] On verify success, store only non-sensitive client state needed by the UI and call `/me` or transition through an authenticated callback; never store OTP or cookie values.
  - [x] Treat 401 from `/me` or verify as session-expired/unauthenticated and keep the user on login with a recoverable message.
- [x] Handle third-party method boundaries (AC: 2, 6)
  - [x] Render Apple and WeChat entries only when returned by `/auth/config`.
  - [x] Do not invent OAuth or Authing routes in this story. If no backend route exists, tapping a third-party entry should show a clear unavailable/coming-soon state while preserving equal visual weight.
  - [x] Keep phone login fully functional; third-party visual entry compliance must not block phone login.
- [x] Add privacy-safe analytics wrapper/events (AC: 5)
  - [x] Add a small frontend analytics service or hook with semantic events such as `auth_view`, `auth_method_tap`, `auth_otp_start`, `auth_otp_verify_success`, `auth_otp_verify_fail`, `auth_privacy_open`, and `auth_terms_open`.
  - [x] Event payloads may include method, result, reason code, and source page; they must not include phone, OTP, raw captcha token, cookie, session id, or BYOK/key material.
  - [x] Keep analytics transport stubbed or no-op if no provider SDK exists yet; the UI should call one wrapper rather than scatter provider-specific calls.
- [x] Add focused tests and verification (AC: 1-6)
  - [x] Add component or DOM tests for method ordering/equal controls, privacy/terms links, OTP cooldown, captcha-required, verify success, and session-expired/error states.
  - [x] Add a mobile viewport smoke check for the login screen if the chosen test stack supports it.
  - [x] Run `pnpm -F nomad-mobile build` and the package typecheck.
  - [x] Run `pnpm ci:auth` to confirm Story 1.1 backend auth contract still passes.
  - [x] Run `pnpm -r build` before marking the story ready for review.

### Review Findings

- [x] [Review][Patch] Method taps must not refetch auth config or re-emit `auth_view` [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:85]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Captcha retry must not hardcode the local stub token as the production flow [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:283]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Captcha-required and retry states must respect cooldown and block repeated uncaptchaed OTP starts [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:148]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Phone changes must clear stale OTP/captcha/cooldown state before verification [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:247]
- [x] [Review][Patch] New OTP sends should clear any previous OTP value before cooldown starts [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:154]
- [x] [Review][Patch] iOS method ordering must place methods missing from `ios_equal_weight_order` after configured methods [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:50]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Login method labels should honor `method.label` from `/auth/config` with safe fallbacks [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:231]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Legal links must handle missing URLs and reject unsafe URL schemes instead of silently doing nothing [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:117]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Analytics failures and sensitive key aliases must not break login or leak PII [apps/mobile/src/auth/analytics.ts:16]
- [x] [Review][Patch] `Retry-After` parsing must handle nested error details and HTTP-date values [apps/mobile/src/auth/api.ts:41]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Successful `/me` followed by an `onAuthenticated` callback error must not be reported as OTP verification failure [apps/mobile/src/auth/LoginScreen.tsx:181]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Device fingerprint should remain stable within the page when `localStorage` is unavailable [apps/mobile/src/auth/device.ts:8]
- [x] [Review][Patch] `nomad-mobile` should declare its generated types package dependency instead of importing across package boundaries implicitly [apps/mobile/src/auth/api.ts:1]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Method row layout should adapt when fewer than three login methods are enabled [apps/mobile/src/styles.css:128]

## Dev Notes

### Current Project State

- There is currently no `apps/mobile` package. The workspace already includes `apps/*`, so create the mobile app in `apps/mobile` and keep it in the existing pnpm monorepo.
- Architecture permits "React Native / RN WebView or mobile Web, chosen during implementation." For this MVP story, mobile Web with Vite + React + TypeScript is the smallest compatible path unless the repo gains a mobile package before development starts.
- `docs/api/openapi.yaml` is the API source of truth. Generated API types live in `packages/types/src/api-types.ts`; do not hand-edit generated types.
- The old `AGENTS.md` `.bmad-core` instructions are legacy residue. BMAD 6.8 artifacts in `_bmad/`, `_bmad-output/`, and `.agents/skills/` are authoritative.

### Auth Contract From Story 1.1

- Config: `GET /auth/config` returns `privacy_url`, `user_agreement_url`, `enabled_methods`, `ios_equal_weight_order`, and `captcha`.
- OTP start: `POST /auth/otp/start` accepts `phone`, optional `region`, and optional `captcha_token`; it returns `sent`, `retry_after_sec`, `captcha_required`, and optional `captcha_provider`.
- OTP verify: `POST /auth/otp/verify` accepts either `{ phone, otp }` or `{ phone, code }`, plus optional `device_fingerprint`/`device_id`; use `otp` as the primary client field.
- Verify success returns `user_id`, `user`, and `session`, sets the httpOnly `sid` cookie, and emits `X-Device-Id`.
- `/me` is the current-user/session check. It requires the session cookie and returns 401 when unauthenticated or expired.
- Local auth is a testable stub: OTP start must happen before verify; default stub OTP is `000000`; default captcha stub token is `captcha-ok`; sessions are in-memory.

### UX Requirements

- Mobile-only, single-column first screen.
- Touch targets must be >=44pt; transitions should stay in the 120-200ms range.
- First screen must visibly expose privacy policy and user agreement links.
- iOS China login entries must be same layer, equal weight, same size, and ordered Apple, phone, WeChat when enabled.
- Login states to cover: initial, config loading, OTP sent, resend cooldown, captcha required, request error, verify error, success, and session expired.
- Failure states should surface useful help and allow retry without clearing all user input.

### Analytics and Privacy Requirements

- Login first-screen analytics must distinguish entry methods.
- Use snake_case event names and one wrapper service/hook.
- Never log or emit phone number, OTP, raw captcha token, cookie value, session id, or secrets.
- If Sentry or an analytics SDK is not configured in this story, keep the wrapper no-op or console-disabled in production rather than introducing provider-specific coupling.

### Latest Technical Notes

- Vite 8 docs list the `react-ts` template and require Node.js 20.19+ or 22.12+; the WSL environment has Node v22.22.1, so the current runtime satisfies Vite's requirement.
- Vite treats `index.html` as the app entry and supports monorepo dependency resolution, which fits `apps/mobile`.
- React's official RSC advisory affects React Server Components packages in vulnerable 19.x lines. This story should build a client-only login screen and must not add React Server Components packages or RSC bundler plugins.

### Project Structure Notes

- Expected new files include `apps/mobile/package.json`, `apps/mobile/index.html`, `apps/mobile/src/main.tsx`, `apps/mobile/src/App.tsx`, login components, API client, analytics wrapper, tests, and mobile CSS.
- Expected existing files to inspect before edits: root `package.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `docs/api/openapi.yaml`, and `packages/types/src/api-types.ts`.
- Do not modify backend auth routes unless implementation reveals an API contract mismatch; if that happens, update OpenAPI first, regenerate types, and rerun Story 1.1 auth verification.

### Completion Notes List

- Created `apps/mobile` as a Vite 8 + React 19 + TypeScript mobile Web package inside the existing pnpm workspace.
- Implemented the mobile login first screen with `/auth/config` loading, iOS equal-weight method ordering, phone OTP start/verify, captcha-required retry via an injectable captcha token provider with a dev-only local fallback, httpOnly-cookie-compatible `credentials: "include"` API calls, `/me` session verification, and recoverable error states.
- Added non-blocking privacy/user-agreement link handling that preserves current input state.
- Added a privacy-safe auth analytics wrapper and login events without phone, OTP, captcha token, cookie, session id, or secret payloads.
- Added focused Vitest/Testing Library coverage for method ordering/equal controls, legal links, OTP cooldown, captcha-required retry, verify success, API retry parsing, device fallback stability, PII-safe analytics, and session-expired handling.
- Resolved all BMAD code review patch findings and moved Story 1.2 to done.
- Dev server HTTP smoke passed with a local 200 response; browser automation screenshot was not completed because the in-app browser control runtime was not available in this session.
